articles.author: The Associated Press
articles.description: The U.S. and Israel killed Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in strikes on Saturday. President Donald Trump called on Iranians to “take back” their country.
articles.title: Live updates: Iran's future unclear after Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in US-Israeli attack

articles.author: Bridget Brown, Lorian Belanger, Ricardo Benassi, Hrvoje Hranjski
articles.description: President Donald Trump said "there will likely be more" U.S. service members killed in Iran before the conflict that killed Supreme Leader  Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ends.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump says ‘there will likely be more’ US service members killed in Iran before conflict ends

articles.author: Bridget Brown, Lorian Belanger, Ricardo Benassi, Hrvoje Hranjski
articles.description: President Donald Trump said "there will likely be more" U.S. service members killed in Iran before the conflict that killed Supreme Leader  Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ends.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump says ‘there will likely be more’ US service members killed in Iran before conflict ends

articles.author: Bridget Brown, Lorian Belanger, Ricardo Benassi, Hrvoje Hranjski
articles.description: President Donald Trump said "there will likely be more" U.S. service members killed in Iran before the conflict that killed Supreme Leader  Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ends.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump says ‘there will likely be more’ US service members killed in Iran before conflict ends

articles.author: Ben Finley
articles.description: Three American service members have been killed and five others seriously wounded during the U.S. military’s attacks on Iran. Those are the first American casualties in the offensive that's sparked retaliation from the Islamic Republic. U.S. Central Command announced the deaths Sunday in a post on X but didn't say when and where they occurred. The statement also says “several others sustained minor shrapnel injuries and concussions” and were going to return to duty. President Donald Trump told the Daily Mail on Sunday that the American service members who were killed were “great people.” He also said the conflict could last for “four weeks or so.”
articles.title: 3 US troops killed and 5 are seriously wounded in Iran operation

articles.author: Josef Federman, David Klepper
articles.description: The barrage of U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran came so quickly that they were nearly simultaneous — with three strikes in three locations hitting within a minute. They killed Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and some 40 senior figures. That's according to an Israeli military official who spoke on condition of anonymity to more fully detail the attack. A person familiar with the operation said that, before the attacks, the CIA had for months tracked the movements of senior Iranian leaders, including the country’s supreme leader. The intelligence was shared with Israeli officials, and the timing of the strikes was adjusted in part because of that information.
articles.title: CIA tracked Khamenei and other Iranian leaders for months before US-Israeli attacks

articles.author: Associated Press
articles.description: The Associated Press is using the word “war” to refer to the joint U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran and Iran’s retaliation. This reflects the scope and intensity of the fighting. The United States and Israel targeted key military targets and killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other government leaders. Iran responded by launching missiles and drones targeting Israel and several Gulf Arab states that host U.S. armed forces. Remaining leaders have vowed revenge over Khamenei’s death. The strikes and counterattacks indicate that the killing of Khamenei, and Trump’s calls for the overthrow of the decades-old Islamic Republic, could lead to prolonged conflict that could envelop the Middle East.
articles.title: How AP decided to describe US-Israeli strikes on Iran, and Iran’s retaliation, as war

articles.author: Brian Melley
articles.description: Iranian missiles and drones have hit the United Arab Emirates, shaking Dubai’s image as a safe, tax-free haven for foreigners. Officials say debris from intercepted weapons sparked fires at major landmarks. A person was killed at Abu Dhabi’s airport, and seven others were injured. The Defense Ministry says air defenses had dealt with hundreds of missiles and drones over two days. Videos show fires near luxury hotels. Officials have praised their air defenses and say they will work hard to keep people safe. Visitors describe hearing constant blasts, and some say they will reconsider returning.
articles.title: Dubai's image as a haven is rocked by Iranian airstrikes

articles.author: Cathy Bussewitz
articles.description: Countries that are part of the OPEC+ oil cartel have announced they will boost production of crude oil. The decision came after U.S. and Israeli forces launched a major attack on Iran and the country responded with strikes on Israel and U.S. military installations around the Gulf, disrupting oil shipments from the region. Attacks throughout the region could restrict countries’ abilities to export oil to the rest of the world. Energy experts say that could lead to higher prices for crude oil and gasoline.
articles.title: OPEC boosts oil production after US and Israeli attacks

articles.author: Amir-hussein Radjy, Sarah El Deeb, Kareem Chehayeb, Lee Keath
articles.description: Some of the jubilation was open and even raucous. People danced in Iranian streets, honked car horns in celebration and screamed joyfully from windows and rooftops over the killing of the country’s supreme leader. But as bombardment by the United States and Israel fell from the air for a second day Sunday, many expressed fear and uncertainty over what direction Iran will take. The death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who stood at the pinnacle of Iran’s Islamic Republic for nearly four decades, along with a number of top military leaders in the first day of the U.S.-Israeli campaign, stunned Iranians and stirred a mix of complex emotions in a divided nation.
articles.title: Some celebration, deep uncertainty in Iran after strikes and leader's death
